MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. John Bradley 408-998-2000 appeared for assignee Landmark Collection. / Atty. Mark Romeo of Law Offices of Mark Romeo 415-395-9315 appeared for defendant.

Hearing Held - The Court modified the tentative ruling as follows: ASSIGNEE LANDMARK COLLECTION SERVICES INC.'s Motion For Order Enforcing Civil Subpoenas Duces Tecum Directed To JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. And First Tennessee Bank.

Motion denied without prejudice. It appears that any interest Landmark has in the disputed information will be available through the family court case, and further information is an invasion of Ms. Paul's privacy. Contrary to Landmark's argument, Williams v. Superior Court considers financial details to be private, and in such cases there must be some showing of compelling need, which is absent here. If requested information is not available to Landmark in family division it may re-apply here. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Julia Campins, Clerk K.Liu, not reported. (D302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES: Cause comes on for further court trial in Dept 611.

Mr. Brooks advised the court that he would not put on any rebuttal witnesses.
The parties and Court agreed to the following briefing schedule:
Initial closing briefs: to be served and filed, with courtesy copy delivered to Dept. 611, by 4:00pm on November 1, 2004;
Opposition briefs: to be served and filed, with courtesy copy delivered to Dept. 611, by 4:00pm on November 22, 2004; however if Mr. Carroll is in trial in Napa in the matter of Media Room vs Fitzsimmons on that date, the opposition brief shall be due on December 6, 2004;

The parties stipulated to the return of exhibits, and an order so providing was signed in open court. Mr. Carroll took custody of the exhibits except for exhibits 14A, 79A, and the transcipts from the deposition of Michael Nadelman, which were retained by the Court.
